submission.questionnaireText:
Lindsay Syed
Adult Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
09/29/2025 at 4:55 AM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(1) I do not experience this
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Around the belly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me

(1) I do not experience this

6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

I do not wake up when sleeping

I do not wake up when sleeping
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
I do not wake up when sleeping
11. Do you NOT dream?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this

(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me

Not Applicable
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(1) I do not experience this
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(1) I do not experience this
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
Not Applicable
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.

sugar and carbs after breastfeeding
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
No
34. Do you experience constipation?
(1) I do not experience this
35. Do you experience nausea?

36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(1) I do not experience this

(1) I do not experience this
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(1) I do not experience this
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
No
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(1) I do not experience this
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(1) I do not experience this
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(1) I do not experience this
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
breastfeeding only since 9 weeks
symptoms are due to pregnancy, birth, breastfeeding; nervous system instable, tiredness, 0 libido, dry skin on legs and arms , suddenly visible veins on hands,- never had this before.
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
vit c
vit e
collagen
gelatine
sometimes taurin
regular basis liver and oyster supplements
sometimes hydrogen supplement
progesterone now and then
aloe vera juice daily
bone broth 3-4x a week
electrolytes
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
No
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
No
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
mornings: tea (loose leafs) and then smoothie with fruits, collagen, honey, electrolytes

lunch vegs & protein carbs

ev as well or chicken or oxtail soups

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
currently new mum:) lack of sleep, havent done sport in months, normally tennis, padel etc

work depended on th weeks, office/sales job fintech area

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
optimise all my areas where i have symptomd

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
no, not aware

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
1.77m

64kg (befpre pregnancy 59)

9weeeks postpartum, was 80kg with baby

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
eat nutrient dense, bio quality, farmer etc

submission.advisorNotesText:
Appointment #1
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
1:00 PM – 2:00 PM
Progress Note 
Lyndsay is a new mom that is currently breast feeding (for 9 weeks) and is looking to optimize her overall well-being. The main things she’d like to work on:

Energy levels

Anxiety

Agitation

Brain fog

Lack of sex drive

Brittle nails

Heavy metal burden (was working on this prior to pregnancy so wants to continue with this)

As we know the process of giving birth dumps a lot of progesterone and minerals, so she’s likely deficient in both of those things. I instructed her on the call to start using progesterone on her gums more frequently if needed, so hopefully that will help mitigate some of these issues.

Her diet is very clean, although recently she was told to stop consuming raw dairy products because of behavioral issues with her baby in the form of tantrums. A functional medicine doctor told Lindsay that he (her baby) was reacting to dairy products and that’s why he was acting like this. She did say that she gave this up for a few weeks but she’s feeling very deprived/depleted because this was a main source of her calories. She did introduce it again, and her baby did have a tantrum but we both agreed that we don’t think this is due to the dairy, but most likely due to Lindsays stress/anxiety. I talked to her about trying goat milk products, and she will look into this but she would prefer to start drinking raw bovine milk again. I am totally ok with this if everyone else is on board, I feel like she needs the fat and calcium! Also, if we have any literature on the benefits of raw vs. pasteurized dairy, please attach it. She’s been struggling with family members and doctors who are convinced this is dangerous. I will also send her some info, but if anyone else has anything let’s send as much as we can!

Energy Levels:

This is mostly due to having a baby and lacking sleep, but we also did go through her dietary intake and she is not getting enough calories. I am providing snacks and balanced meal suggestions in the messaging, but if we could attach some of our content that would be great. Other things that are noticeably helpful are taurine and glycine. I wasn’t sure about taurine with breastfeeding, but as long as we believe this is ok I think she should take this on a more consistent basis.

Anxiety:

Again this seemed to be related to her not eating in a balanced fashion. We discussed making sure she’s eating well-balanced meals that are dense as well as snacks that include fat/protein and a carb. She’s been mostly snacking on things like dates, so I asked her to add some butter or cheese to that, or have it alongside a hard-boiled egg. I also think this is a low-progesterone reaction, so I asked her each time she feels this to rub a drop or two of progeste-E on her gums. I also educated her on the use of chamomile tea here for both it’s (apiginen) estrogen lowering and anxiety lowering properties.

Agitation:

Again a nutrient deficiency. Let’s get some liquid/ or multiminerals on board for her along with her diet changes.

Brain Fog:

This is correlated with reduced sleep and she is trying to nap when her baby is napping, but sometimes she simply can’t get enough sleep. I think nourishing herself a bit more and upping her calories will likely help with this as well.

Lack of Sex Drive:

Another indication of low progesterone post delivery. This will likely take time for hormones to normalize, but using the progest E consistently and getting more fat in her diet should help.

Brittle Nails:

She’s getting 2-3 scoops of collagen and taking glycine each day, so this is more likely due to the zinc deficiency associates with post-delivery. The mineral supplement with address this.

Heavy Metal Burden:

This was being addressed with binders and a whole protocol prior to her pregnancy. I’m not real keen on kicking things up during breast feeding, but I did educate her on silica rich fruits and veggies as well as using cilantro, as these things have a very strong affinity for heavy metals. Raw dairy is also great at reducing lead and cadmium, so hopefully she can get back on that. She also doing daily aloe vera which is great for the emodin and the detox properties.

Current Supplements:

vit c
vit e
collagen
gelatine
sometimes taurin
regular basis liver and oyster supplements
sometimes hydrogen supplement
progesterone now and then (uses a few drops when stressed)
aloe vera juice daily
bone broth 3-4x a week
electrolytes

Calculated calories: 2,525 per day.

Macro Breakdown: P/C/F should be 130g/275g/100g

Plan:

Any info on raw vs. pasteurized dairy much appreciated!

Sample meals/snacks/recipes.

Multiminerals supplement: she’s not in the US so iHerb

Progest-E: use 1-2 drops when feel anxiety, stress or inability to sleep.

Calories: increase to 2,500+ per day. 130 grams of protein, 275 grams of carbs and 100 grams of fat to fuel breast feeding and daily activities.

I provided sources of improved snacks in the chat, as well as chamomile tea, sources of silica and cilantro for heavy metal detox.

Any testing that we would want besides thyroid? It seems like she has access to testing and I don’t think it would be a bad idea to at least get her thyroid tested given her exposure to heavy metals in the past.

I’m honestly at a loss here…..she’s already eating well, already taking a lot of supplements and I am hesitant to implement anything else given that she’s breast feeding!

---


Chart Note
Clinician: Maria Dees
Edit  
4:05 PM
Lindsay Syed sent message on October 2, 2025 at 2:38 PM
To be added in my symptoms: brittle nails collagen loss
